reasoning: The forecast condition is whether the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee declares by the end of 2025 that the US entered a recession in 2023 or 2024. As of November 2025, no such declaration has been made. The US experienced positive economic and GDP growth throughout 2023 and 2024, defying early predictions of a downturn. Recessions typically involve significant contractions in GDP and employment, which did not occur during the relevant time period. The Manifold Markets estimate for this event sits at an overwhelmingly confident 1%. Given this lack of evidence of an actual recession, it is exceedingly unlikely that the NBER will retroactively declare a 2023/2024 recession in the final weeks of 2025. Thus, I estimate a 2% chance, near the absolute minimum.
